description: Bacteriophage phi80 bacterial attachment site attB; the E. coli chromosomal target within the 3′ end of yciI (beside tonB) for phi80 integrase + IHF.

references.authors: Leong JM, Nunes-Düby S, Lesser CF, Youderian P, Susskind MM, Landy A

references.title: Conditional-replication, integration, excision, and retrieval plasmid-host systems for gene structure-function studies of bacteria

references.authors: St-Pierre F, Cui L, Priest DG, Endy D, Dodd IB, Shearwin KE
references.title: One-step cloning and chromosomal integration of DNA

label: phi80_attB
note: Phage phi80 bacterial attachment site attB, within the 3′ end of gene yciI (b1251), divergent from and adjacent to tonB — reconciling the historical 'tonB region / ~28 min' lore (beside tonB, not in it; not a tRNA gene). 54 bp window from NC_000913.3 containing the 15 bp core consensus AACACTTTCTTAAAT; the exact 17 bp common core and crossover (Leong et al. 1985) are behind paywalls and the precise boundaries are PROVISIONAL. Cognate phage site: phi80_attP.

provenance.sequence_source: NC_000913.3 (E. coli K-12 MG1655), 3′ end of yciI (b1251), adjacent to tonB, positions 1310549..1310602 (+ strand), 100% identity over 54 bp
